name: Collado del Aire – circular desde El Campillo de Purchena
region_intro: Mountain bike trails Purchena are set within the diverse landscapes of the Almanzora Valley, at the foot of the Sierra de los Filabres. This region is characterized by its abundant forests and green areas, offering a contrast to more arid surroundings. The area features natural enclaves like the Gevas Ravine and the wooded area of Campillo, alongside the scenic Vía Verde del Almanzora, which follows an old railway line. These varied terrains provide a compelling destination for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Purchena The most popular mountain bike trail is Collado del Aire – circular desde El Campillo de Purchena, a 16.1 miles (25.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gain through the Sierra de los Filabres. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Mirador de la Virgen – World's largest mortar loop from Olula del Río, a difficult 24.4 miles (39.3 km) path. This trail features substantial climbs and panoramic views across the region. Local mountain bikers also love the Cantoria Tunnel – Almanzora Station loop from Macael, a 29.4 miles (47.3 km) trail leading through the Almanzora Valley, often completed in about 2 hours 55 minutes. Mountain biking around Purchena is defined by forested mountain slopes, the scenic Almanzora River Valley, and dedicated paths like the Vía Verde del Almanzora. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Purchena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Purchena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Purchena?
A: Purchena offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 40 routes available. These trails cater to various skill levels, including 3 easy, 27 moderate, and 12 difficult options, ensuring there's something for every rider.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for mountain biking in Purchena?
A: Mountain biking in Purchena ranges from moderate to difficult. While there are a few easier routes, the majority of trails are classified as moderate or difficult, often featuring significant elevation changes and varied terrain through the Sierra de los Filabres and Almanzora Valley.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Purchena?
A: Yes, Purchena offers several circular mountain bike routes. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017955653/collado-del-aire-circular-desde-el-campillo-de-purchena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado del Aire – circular desde El Campillo de Purchena&lt;/a&gt; is a 25.9 km loop. Another popular option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31459850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cantoria Tunnel – Almanzora Station loop from Macael&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 47.3 km through the Almanzora Valley.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Purchena?
A: The terrain around Purchena is diverse, characterized by forested mountain slopes of the Sierra de los Filabres, scenic sections along the Almanzora River Valley, and dedicated paths like the Vía Verde del Almanzora. You'll encounter a mix of natural trails, old railway lines, and paths through green areas and ravines.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Purchena?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde del Almanzora is an excellent option for families. This ecotourism route follows an old railway line, offering a mostly flat and well-signposted path that is accessible to various fitness levels, making it ideal for a relaxed family ride.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints accessible by mountain bike in Purchena?
A: The region boasts several scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4625289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Piedra Lobera&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31456786" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Virgen – World's largest mortar loop from Olula del Río&lt;/a&gt; route is known for its substantial climbs and expansive regional vistas.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural monuments to see along the trails?
A: Yes, you can discover several natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2650515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Huevo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural monument. Additionally, you might encounter gorges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5315245" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Estrecho de Urrácal&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2649812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cerrada de Valentín (La Cerrá), Tíjola&lt;/a&gt;, or even the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2650529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de la Paloma&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Purchena?
A: Many routes in Purchena integrate with the region's history. The Vía Verde del Almanzora, for instance, follows an old railway line, featuring historical elements like old viaducts and tunnels. You can also pass by sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5037637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aqueduct near Purchena&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5306706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Tíjola train Station&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Purchena?
A: The mountain bike trails around Purchena are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have explored the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and well-maintained paths.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Purchena?
A: Purchena offers good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for riding. While summer can be hot, the region's forested areas and higher elevations in the Sierra de los Filabres can offer some respite.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that follow old railway lines?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde del Almanzora is a significant route that follows an old railway line. It offers a unique experience, combining natural tranquility with regional history, and is known for its relatively flat and well-signposted path. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38151974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aqueduct near Purchena – Old Tíjola train Station loop from Estación de Fines-Olula&lt;/a&gt; also incorporate sections of this historical infrastructure.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through forests or green areas?
A: Absolutely. Purchena is known for its abundant forests and green areas, particularly in the Sierra de los Filabres. Many routes, such as those near the wooded area of Campillo, will take you through lush landscapes, offering a refreshing contrast to more arid surroundings.
tips: Port with easy access and a very good track near the Collado del Aire.
The Urrácal antennas are in turn a beautiful viewpoint with views of the Almanzora valley.

waypoints.name: View of the Almanzora Valley from the Urrácal Antennas
